title: Codex Mendoza: The Tribute Lists of the Aztec Empire
description: A post-conquest codex painted by Aztec artists showing the extent of the empire — the tribute paid by each conquered province: jaguar skins, eagle feathers, gold, cacao, jade.
introduction: A post-conquest codex painted by Aztec artists showing the extent of the empire — the tribute paid by each conquered province: jaguar skins, eagle feathers, gold, cacao, jade.

translation.text: The province of Tochtepec shall send annually: 40 large mantles, 400 loads of cacao, 2 jade necklaces, 1 jade lip-plug, 20 bags of cochineal dye, 800 bundles of quetzal feathers, 1 live eagle.
